titleInfo.title: Kudara Futsuritsuzō to ikkō sanzon keishiki: Gatapu-ri haijishi shutsudo Kondō Futsuritsuzō o megutte
百濟佛立像と一光三尊形式──佳塔里廢寺址出土金銅佛立像をめぐって──
Statue of Standing Buddha of Paekche Dynasty and Ikkō Sanzon (Statue of Buddha Triad with One Halo) Style

mods.abstract: Regarding the transcription of 佳塔里 as Gatapu-ri: 1) Until now, we have not discovered any source that mentions the japanese reading of 가탑리 佳塔里 [Gatap-ri]. Therefore, the rendering as Gatapu-ri was created by us ourselves. 2) The Japanese transcription of Korean places follows this general rule: For contemporary materials, use a transcription based partly on Korean reading (i.e. from Hangul). For colonial era materials, use a transription based on Japanese reading (i.e. from Kanji/Hanja). 평양 平壌 [Pyongyang]: Pyon’yan (ピョンヤン) for comtemporary usage, Heijō (へいじょう) for colonial era materials. 경기도 京畿道 [Gyeonggi-do]: Kyongi-dō (キョンギ‐どう) for comtemporary usage, Keiki-dō (けいき‐どう) for colonial era materials. See also p.23 in: https://www.eastasianlib.org/ctp/Subcommittees/sub_JpnRT/RT_Revision_Complete_Draft%20-%202018_Mar27.pdf. 3) Since this is a text from 1977, we use the transcription for contemporary usage. If 佳塔里 would have been mentioned in colonial era materials, the reading would probably be Katō-ri(?) (かとう‐り). There are indeed several materials from the colonial era that mention 佳塔里. I have however, not found any materials that used Furigana in order to show the japanese reading of 佳塔里, therefore I cannot for 100% guarantee that Katō-ri is the correct rendering. Other japanese readings for 佳: Go-on: け (ke); Kan-on: かい (kai); Kan’yō-on: けい (kei). 4) Normally, the reading of the korean reading 가탑리 has to be transcribed as follows: 가탑리 → Gatapri → Gatamni → ガタムニ → Gatamuni (Hangul → Revised Romanisation → Assimilation of consonants “pr” to “mn” → Rendering into Katakana → Transcription into the Hepburn-System). However, since this is a geographic name and ri 리/里 is an administrative unit, a hyphen is inserted between tap 탑 and ri 리; the assimilation of the consonants [pr → mn] will be ignored. See p.24 in: https://www.geschkult.fu-berlin.de/e/oas/korea-studien/files/romanization.pdf. Furthermore as in the other geographic cases such as in 京畿道 [Gyeonggi-do], we’ll add a hyphen into the japanese romanization as well. Therefore: 가탑리 → Gatapri → Gatap-ri → ガタプ‐リ → Gatapu-ri. 5) We have rendered p-ri in Katakana as プリ (pu-ri) and not as ッリ (r-ri). For one, we have followed to ignore the assimilation of consonants as in the korean case when an hyphen is added. Furthermore, in japanese the combination of the two consontants p and r is rendered into pu プ and r+vowel in most of the cases (always?). See for example the japanese transcriptions of foreign terms with the combination of the two consonants “pr”, such as: epuron エプロン [Apron], konpuressā コンプレッサー [Compressor], Kipurosu キプロス [Cyprus], puraibēto プライベート [private], purintā プリンター [Printer], Puroisen プロイセン [Preußen], puroresu プロレス [Pro Wrestling], purosesu プロセス [Process], purezentēshon プレゼンテーション [Presentation], etc. or the multiple renderings of the river Dnieper (Днепр, Дніпро, Дняпро), such as Doniepuru-gawa ドニエプル川, Donepuru-gawa ドネプル川, Donyepuru-gawaドニェプル川, Donyēpuru-gawa ドニェープル川, Donipuro-gawa ドニプロ川.

titleInfo.title: Museum ：東京国立博物館研究誌
